description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a very small proof-of-concept log-analysis exercise for a new junior teammate.  
  A sample application log already exists on the machine; you only need to analyse it and produce a concise summary file for verification.

  1. Log location  
     • Full path : /home/user/app/logs/app.log  
     • The file already exists; do **not** edit it.  

  2. What you must create  
     • A new text file called **test_summary.txt** in the same directory:  
       /home/user/app/logs/test_summary.txt  

  3. What the summary file must contain  
     • Exactly 8 lines, no extra blank lines or spaces at the start/end of any line.  
     • Fixed order and spelling shown below.  
       
       Line 1 REPORT_GENERATED=YYYY-MM-DD  
              – Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s date in the container’s local time, using four-digit year, two-digit month, two-digit day (e.g. 2024-04-07).  
       Line 2 ERROR_COUNT=<number>  
       Line 3 WARN_COUNT=<number>  
       Line 4 INFO_COUNT=<number>  
       Line 5 --LAST_3_ERRORS--  
       Line 6 1. <full log line of the most recent ERROR>  
       Line 7 2. <full log line of the 2nd most recent ERROR>  
       Line 8 3. <full log line of the 3rd most recent ERROR>  

     • “Most recent” is determined purely by timestamp order in app.log (the file is already sorted by time).  
     • Keep the exact text of each error line, including the leading index (“1. ”, “2. ”, “3. ”).  

  4. Verification expectations (what the script will check)  
     • Line 2 must show the total count of lines in app.log containing “[ERROR]”.  
     • Line 3 must show the total count of lines containing “[WARN]”.  
     • Line 4 must show the total count of lines containing “[INFO]”.  
     • Lines 6-8 must list the latest three error lines in reverse-chronological order (newest first).  
       All spacing and characters must be identical to the corresponding lines found in app.log, except for the prefixed “1. ”, “2. ”, “3. ”.  

  5. Hints (you are free to ignore them)  
     • grep, wc -l, tail, awk, cat, head, sort can all help.  
     • You should only need three or four commands in total.  

  When you are done, /home/user/app/logs/test_summary.txt must exist and conform exactly to the 8-line layout above so that the automated grader can validate it.
truth:
<preexisting>
    <!-- Directory and file that already exist before the agent starts -->
    Directory: /home/user/app/logs
    File: /home/user/app/logs/app.log
    Contents:
      2023-09-15 10:15:21 [INFO] Application started
      2023-09-15 10:15:22 [INFO] Loading configuration
      2023-09-15 10:15:23 [WARN] Deprecated config option used
      2023-09-15 10:15:24 [ERROR] Failed to connect to database
      2023-09-15 10:15:25 [INFO] Retrying connection
      2023-09-15 10:15:26 [ERROR] Failed to connect to database
      2023-09-15 10:15:27 [WARN] Using fallback settings
      2023-09-15 10:15:28 [INFO] Starting processing loop
      2023-09-15 10:16:00 [INFO] Processed 100 records
      2023-09-15 10:16:05 [ERROR] Unexpected null pointer
      2023-09-15 10:16:10 [INFO] Shutting down
      2023-09-15 10:16:11 [INFO] Application stopped
      2023-09-16 09:00:00 [INFO] Application started
      2023-09-16 09:00:01 [WARN] Low disk space
      2023-09-16 09:00:02 [ERROR] Disk write failed
      2023-09-16 09:00:03 [INFO] Cleanup complete
  </preexisting>

  <expected-summary>
    Path: /home/user/app/logs/test_summary.txt
    Fixed content that MUST be present (line numbers correspond to the public spec):
      Line 2: ERROR_COUNT=4
      Line 3: WARN_COUNT=3
      Line 4: INFO_COUNT=9
      Line 5: --LAST_3_ERRORS--
      Line 6: 1. 2023-09-16 09:00:02 [ERROR] Disk write failed
      Line 7: 2. 2023-09-15 10:16:05 [ERROR] Unexpected null pointer
      Line 8: 3. 2023-09-15 10:15:26 [ERROR] Failed to connect to database
    Line 1 will be accepted if it matches
      REPORT_GENERATED=\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}
  </expected-summary>
name: task_000000_9638a67d
